attack_chain.description: A former (sacked) IT employee used stolen/retained AWS credentials to authenticate into the victim organization's AWS environment after termination.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Root Cause: Stolen credentials by former employee

attack_chain.description: After gaining access using the stolen credentials, the former employee deleted 23 AWS servers, destroying the organization's infrastructure.

initial_access_vector: Former employee retained or stole valid AWS credentials after being terminated (sacked), then used those credentials to log into the organization's AWS environment.
persistence_mechanism: No explicit persistence mechanism described; attacker leveraged pre-existing valid cloud account credentials.
privilege_escalation: No privilege escalation described; the former IT employee already had sufficient permissions via their existing (or stolen) account to delete servers.

nodes.description: A former (sacked) IT employee used stolen/retained AWS credentials to authenticate into the victim organization's AWS environment after termination.
nodes.evidence_quote: Root Cause: Stolen credentials by former employee

nodes.description: After gaining access using the stolen credentials, the former employee deleted 23 AWS servers, destroying the organization's infrastructure.

evidence_quotes: Root Cause: Stolen credentials by former employee
